sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Benfica are in excellent form, winning their last three matches with an impressive goal tally of 11-0, showcasing their attacking strength and defensive solidity. Bayer 04 Leverkusen, on the other hand, have had mixed results, with a heavy 2-7 loss to PSG in their recent Champions League fixture and inconsistent performances, managing only one win in their last five games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters in 2014, Benfica and Bayer Leverkusen have a balanced record with one draw (0-0) and one win for Leverkusen (3-1). These results suggest tight contests, with Leverkusen having a slight edge in converting chances.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Benfica's season stats show struggles in the Champions League, sitting at 35th with no points and only 2 goals scored, though their domestic form is strong. Leverkusen, at 29th, have also underperformed with just 2 points and a leaky defense, conceding 10 goals in 3 matches, indicating vulnerabilities that Benfica could exploit.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Benfica, Nicolás Otamendi and Vangelis Pavlidis are in top form, with Pavlidis netting 6 goals in recent matches. Leverkusen’s Alejandro Grimaldo, with 3 goals and 2 assists in his last 5 games, remains a threat, especially given his history of performing well against Benfica.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Benfica's high corner count (7 per game, 3rd in league rank) and ability to create big chances could pressure Leverkusen’s defense, which has struggled in Europe. Leverkusen’s tendency to score late (40% of goals in the last 15 minutes) might push Benfica to maintain focus throughout the match.

sections.subtitle: Prediction
sections.text: Given Benfica’s superior recent form and home advantage at Estádio da Luz, coupled with Leverkusen’s defensive frailties, a home win seems likely. Sofascore’s 52% probability for a Benfica victory aligns with this assessment, though Leverkusen’s attacking potential could make it close.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Benfica and Bayer 04 Leverkusen at Estádio da Luz ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock. Despite the 0-0 scoreline, the game has been intense, with both teams creating opportunities to score. Benfica appeared more dominant in terms of chances created and territorial control, while Leverkusen held a slight edge in ball possession. The match remains finely poised heading into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Benfica's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Benfica showed significant intent in the first half, creating multiple big chances and getting several shots on target. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area highlight their attacking ambition. However, their inability to convert these opportunities, including missing clear-cut chances and hitting the woodwork, will be a concern. Defensively, they were solid, winning a high percentage of duels and aerial battles, which helped them maintain control for large parts of the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Bayer 04 Leverkusen's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Leverkusen struggled to match Benfica’s intensity in the attacking third during the first half. They managed fewer shots on target and created only one big chance, which they failed to convert. Their possession was slightly higher, but they couldn’t translate it into meaningful penetration, with fewer final third entries and penalty area incursions. Defensively, they were less effective in duels, particularly in the air, and will need to shore up their physical presence to compete in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Benfica largely dictating the attacking momentum with several sustained periods of pressure, especially in the middle and latter stages of the half. Their attacking spells were more frequent and intense, often pushing Leverkusen onto the back foot. Leverkusen had their moments, particularly in brief bursts around the midway point of the half, but their momentum waned as Benfica reasserted control. These shifts suggest Benfica has the upper hand in terms of creating dangerous moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Benfica, Dodi Lukebakio has been a constant threat in the first half with his involvement in dangerous attacks and could be pivotal in breaking the deadlock. Georgiy Sudakov also stood out with his creative contributions in midfield. On the Leverkusen side, Alejandro Grimaldo has been a standout performer with his ability to influence play from deeper positions. Ernest Poku could be key for Leverkusen if they capitalize on any counter-attacking opportunities in the second half.
